programme.title: Spare Parts
programme.desc: Weapons attached to one arm, four young women must fight like gladiators as a bloodthirsty crowd cheers them on.

programme.title: Centigrade
programme.desc: A man and his pregnant wife become trapped in their car when a storm buries them underneath layers of snow and ice. As supplies dwindle and temperatures plunge, the couple must battle the elements and hypothermia in a desperate fight for survival.

programme.title: Come True
programme.desc: Strange events plague a troubled teen when she participates in a scientific experiment to stop her nightmares.

programme.title: Dark Was the Night
programme.desc: When a logging company decimates a forest in a small town, a sheriff (Kevin Durand) and his deputy (Lukas Haas) must confront the violent and unexplainable events that follow.

programme.title: Feral
programme.desc: A wild animal attacks six grad students on a weekend hike in the woods. One by one, they become infected with an unknown virus that turns them into bloodthirsty creatures.

programme.title: Hatching
programme.desc: After finding a wounded bird, a girl brings its strange egg home and nurtures it until it hatches. The creature that emerges soon becomes her closest friend and a living nightmare, plunging her into a twisted reality that her mother refuses to see.

programme.title: Hellbender
programme.desc: A teen and her mother live simply in a home in the woods, spending their time making metal music. A chance encounter with a fellow teen causes her to uncover a connection between her family and witchcraft, which causes a rift with her mother.

programme.title: The Houses October Built 2
programme.desc: Still recovering from their abduction last Halloween, friends head back out on the road in search of the ultimate spooky experience.
When Earth's air becomes unbreathable, a message from the future sends a man on a dangerous mission to an unknown world to save the human race.

programme.title: Airborne
programme.desc: A plane takes off during a snowstorm and soon reports both pilots are dead. As the passengers slowly die off, they wish they had never left the ground.

programme.title: All Hallows' Eve 2
programme.desc: A woman finds a VHS tape that shows a series of gruesome tales.

programme.desc: Seth sees his best friend disappear in a flash of light. Two years later, Seth is still dealing with the fallout and suspicions when a series of grisly murders rocks the community.

programme.desc: A murderous assailant traps three people (Alice Eve, Josh Peck, Brian Geraghty) in a small kiosk containing an automatic teller machine.

programme.title: Kaho Naa Pyaar Hai
programme.desc: A poor singer Rohit and rich Sonia fall in love, are stranded on a deserted island, rise then Rohit dies; Sonia finds a Rohit-lookalike, Raj Chopra.

programme.title: Singham Again
programme.desc: Bajirao Singham's rescue mission for his kidnapped wife turns mythic, echoing Ramayana's spirit. He battles a powerful enemy while uncovering deeper truths about righteousness and love.

programme.title: Aparichit - The Stranger
programme.desc: Ramanujam, a lawyer with multiple personality disorder, becomes a vigilante by night, using the teachings of the Garuda Purana to punish and expose criminals and antisocial elements.

programme.title: Ek Deewane Ki Deewaniyat
programme.desc: When A Powerful Politician Falls For A Strong-Willed Superstar, Their Passionate Romance Quickly Spirals Into A Dangerous Game Of Obsession, Pride, And Heartbreak.

programme.title: Bholaa
programme.desc: After a decade-long prison sentence, an ex-convict endeavours to meet his daughter. However, he faces numerous obstacles during his journey.

programme.title: Spin State
programme.desc: A troubled private investigator with blackouts probes a scientist's break-in, uncovering dangerous links between her estranged husband and his own buried trauma.

programme.title: Agent Vinod
programme.desc: Terror escalates when Agent Vinod finds out that an elite business community is behind the death of his colleague.

programme.title: Turtle
programme.desc: While Ramkaran is forced to churn water from the earth in a drought-struck village, the plight of villagers intensifies as they seek different means to quench their thirst.

programme.title: Kesari
programme.desc: Havildar Ishar Singh leads 21 Sikh soldiers against 10,000 invaders in one of history's greatest last stands-where bravery, sacrifice, and honour rise far above impossible odds.

programme.title: Naach Lucky Naach
programme.desc: Lakshmi's dance dreams clash with her mother's disapproval until Krishna brings unexpected change.

programme.title: Himmatwar
programme.desc: A set of circumstances makes Vasu, a moneylender, save the life of a policeman, Sivaraman Nayak. But that only leads to Vasu appearing on the radar of a gangster and contract killer.

programme.title: Sanam Teri Kasam
programme.desc: When an awkward librarian gets disowned by her family, she unexpectedly finds support from her mysterious, brooding neighbor, sparking an unlikely friendship-and maybe more.

programme.title: Chori Chori Chupke Chupke
programme.desc: Raj and Priya hire Madhubala, a prostitute, as their surrogate. Complications arise when Madhubala falls for Raj and refuses to give up the child, threatening their marriage.

programme.title: Khiladi 786
programme.desc: The underachieving son of a marriage bureau owner attempts to marry a don's daughter to a supposed police officer in an effort to prove his worth to his father.

programme.title: Bruce Lee
programme.desc: Kartik sacrifices his studies for his sister and becomes a stuntman. Often mistaken for an undercover policeman, Kartik helps unearth the evil designs of businessmen Deepak Raj and Jayaraj.

programme.title: Chakravyuh
programme.desc: Adil and Kabir infiltrate a Naxal group, but Kabir's loyalty shifts. Friendship turns deadly as ideals clash, forcing them into a battle neither imagined when they started.

programme.title: Chandu Champion
programme.desc: An extraordinary man faces relentless challenges with unyielding determination. His resilience drives him through adversity, leading him to carve a path toward a historic achievement.

programme.title: Chor Machaaye Shor
programme.desc: A thief pretends to be a cop in order to recover his diamond from a site that has been converted into the police headquarters. Although he becomes a favourite of the ACP, a colleague gets suspicious.
Scientists help government investigate a threat that is beyond understanding.

programme.title: Geeta Govinda
programme.desc: Vijay Govind, a lecturer, wishes to get married at the earliest. He tries to woo Geetha, a level-headed woman, who is not easy to convince.

programme.title: Simmba
programme.desc: Simmba, a police officer, works for Durva, a smuggler. His life takes a turn when he decides to avenge a woman who was assaulted by Durva's brothers.

programme.title: Dhadkan
programme.desc: Akshay Kumar,Shilpa Shetty,Sunil Shetty,Mahima Chaudhry

programme.title: Fukrey 3
programme.desc: Four carefree friends chase quick riches, landing in a whirlwind of hilarious chaos, misadventures, and unpredictable turns that test their friendship and dreams.

programme.title: Table No. 21
programme.desc: Vivaan and Siya join game show and turns into survival challenge.

programme.title: Bharat
programme.desc: In a gripping tale, a young boy vows to keep his family united amidst the chaos of India's independence, facing challenges and discovering the true meaning of love.

programme.title: Jigarwala No.1
programme.desc: Dr Robin comes to India to recover his land from a gangster. He is persuaded by a writer who has a hidden agenda.

programme.title: Gangubai Kathiawadi
programme.desc: A young woman, sold to a brothel, rises from victim to ruler by leveraging her underworld connections, turning the world that once controlled her into her own domain of power.

programme.title: The Last Color
programme.desc: A young tightrope walker befriends a widow who wishes to play Holi but cannot do so.

programme.title: Chakravyuh
programme.desc: Kabir infiltrates a Maoist organisation to help Adil Khan, who is a police officer and his friend.

programme.title: Mere Pyare Prime Minister
programme.desc: Kannu writes a letter to the prime minister of India after his mother is raped.

programme.title: Akaash Vani

programme.title: Vicky Donor
programme.desc: Vicky works as a sperm donor for Dr Baldev Chaddha and boosts the clinic's success.

programme.title: Bob Biswas

programme.desc: Satyaprem tries hard to restore Katha's faith in love and prove himself as a worthy person.

programme.title: Kismat Konnection
programme.desc: An architect and a social activist find themselves connected in a strange way by destiny.

programme.title: Words and Music

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: At the jungle animal hospital in Guatemala, the wards are full of exotic patients, many of them orphans rescued from the illegal pet trade.

programme.title: Life on Earth
programme.desc: Bright blue starfish, crimson feather stars, shell-less snails in designs as extravagant as any Paris fashion show, shrimps of every colour, others that are transparent - just a sample of the animal wonders to be found in a small area of the Great Barrier Reef.

programme.title: Voyages of Discovery
programme.desc: Explorer Paul Rose reveals the real story behind the first ever circumnavigation of the world. Ferdinand Magellan set out 500 years ago to find the westward route to the riches of the Spice Islands. But, contrary to popular perception, he never reached them. Rose explains the dramatic sequence of events that led his scurvy-riddled crew to continue around the world without him. The incredible expedition was laced with bloody mutiny and murder, but its achievement was to fundamentally change the lives of the generations that followed, influencing life even today.

programme.title: Goolagong
programme.desc: Grieving for her father, Evonne rebuilds her life, fires Vic, marries Roger and overcomes injury to win Wimbledon as a mother in a historic victory.

programme.title: Scrublands
programme.desc: At teenager Allen's wake, Martin learns that the boy was not as innocent as first portrayed, with Robbie telling him he was persuaded by Byron Swift to drop minor drug charges against him. Martin is given reason to believe that Sgt Piccini could be right in thinking that her investigation is being thwarted from above.

programme.title: Scrublands
programme.desc: Martin believes he has found the link between the shooting victims and asks for Robbie’s help in accessing the case files. A further clue implicates one of the victims as being behind the murder of the missing couple, and young Jamie admits he has been keeping a horrible secret from everybody.

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Sitcom about an upwardly mobile housewife and her long-suffering husband. Hyacinth has to put her plans for Richard's retirement on hold when Rose starts behaving outrageously.

programme.title: One Foot in the Grave
programme.desc: Victor and Margaret agree to look after Kylie the tortoise whilst Margaret's god daughter Jennifer goes on holiday.

programme.title: Life on Earth
programme.desc: Bright blue starfish, crimson feather stars, shell-less snails in designs as extravagant as any Paris fashion show, shrimps of every colour, others that are transparent - just a sample of the animal wonders to be found in a small area of the Great Barrier Reef.

programme.title: Voyages of Discovery
programme.desc: Explorer Paul Rose reveals the real story behind the first ever circumnavigation of the world. Ferdinand Magellan set out 500 years ago to find the westward route to the riches of the Spice Islands. But, contrary to popular perception, he never reached them. Rose explains the dramatic sequence of events that led his scurvy-riddled crew to continue around the world without him. The incredible expedition was laced with bloody mutiny and murder, but its achievement was to fundamentally change the lives of the generations that followed, influencing life even today.

programme.title: Cumbria's Red Squirrels
programme.desc: Cumbria is one of the last major strongholds for one of the British Isles’ iconic native mammals - red squirrels. An endangered species as well as a national favourite, affectionately known as 'red scamps', these beautiful creatures have lived here for thousands of years. Award-winning Cumbrian film-maker Terry Abraham reveals how Lakeland charities, volunteers, businesses and scientists are pulling together to protect these rare animals.

programme.title: This is BBC Four
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm.

programme.title: This is BBC Four
programme.desc: Daily from 7.00pm.

programme.title: This is BBC Four
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm.

programme.title: Dolly: The Sheep That Changed the World
programme.desc: The story of the scientists who created Dolly, the sheep that changed the world. This documentary tells the full story for the first time with never-before-seen archive, revealing how on a small Scottish farm, a handful of the world’s best genetic scientists worked in secret to crack the holy grail of life: cloning. The story, when it broke, caused a moral panic to sweep the world. But how did it happen? Who was behind it? What was the science? And, ultimately, what is Dolly’s legacy today?

programme.title: BBC Proms
programme.desc: A No 1 album and multiple Grammy wins has put singing sensation Samara Joy on the map, establishing the young American artist as ‘a legend in the making’, regularly compared to Ella Fitzgerald and Sarah Vaughan. This much-anticipated Proms debut introduces audiences to her electric combination of youthful energy and old-soul musical style with classic songs and unique twists on timeless tunes, from bossa nova to the Great American Songbook. She salutes figures including Sarah Vaughan, Ella Fitzgerald, Duke Ellington, Billie Holiday, Charles Mingus and Oscar Peterson. Andi Oliver is the guide for an evening of charisma and musical glamour.

programme.title: Music 625: Stravinsky Conducts Stravinsky
programme.desc: Igor Stravinsky conducts the New Philharmonia Orchestra in performances of his own works Variations in Memory of Aldous Huxley and The Firebird Suite. Introduced by Richard Baker.

programme.title: How to Build a Cathedral
programme.desc: The great cathedrals were the wonders of the medieval world - the tallest buildings since the pyramids and the showpieces of medieval Christianity. Yet they were built at a time when most of us lived in hovels. Architectural historian Jon Cannon explores who the people were that built them and how they were able to achieve such a bold vision.

programme.title: Dolly: The Sheep That Changed the World
programme.desc: The story of the scientists who created Dolly, the sheep that changed the world. This documentary tells the full story for the first time with never-before-seen archive, revealing how on a small Scottish farm, a handful of the world’s best genetic scientists worked in secret to crack the holy grail of life: cloning. The story, when it broke, caused a moral panic to sweep the world. But how did it happen? Who was behind it? What was the science? And, ultimately, what is Dolly’s legacy today?

programme.title: This World
programme.desc: Simon Reeve heads to Vietnam to uncover the stories behind the morning pick-me-up. He travels to the central highlands, where he meets the people who grow, pick and pack coffee.

programme.title: How to Build a Cathedral
programme.desc: The great cathedrals were the wonders of the medieval world - the tallest buildings since the pyramids and the showpieces of medieval Christianity. Yet they were built at a time when most of us lived in hovels. Architectural historian Jon Cannon explores who the people were that built them and how they were able to achieve such a bold vision.

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: The quarter-final stage approaches its close. Two teams who have both won one quarter-final and lost another have one last chance to qualify for the semi-finals. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions.

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Quiz show presented by Victoria Coren in which knowledge will only take you so far, as patience and lateral thinking are also vital. In the second of the quarter-finals, three postgraduate students from Brasenose College, Oxford, pit their wits against a trio of Douglas Adams fans who call themselves the Hitchhikers, in honour of the Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy. They compete to draw together the connections between elements which, at first glance, seem utterly random: P J Tracy, Nicci French, Ellery Queen and Grant Naylor.

programme.title: Cricket: Today at the Test
programme.desc: Day 5 highlights from the third Test match between England and New Zealand.

programme.title: Hockney
programme.desc: Documentary about artist David Hockney, from his early life in working-class Bradford to his relocation to Hollywood, with access to his personal archive of photographs and film.

programme.title: The Interview
programme.desc: David Hockney was one of the world's most successful and influential artists of our time. At 11, he wanted to be an artist. At 87, he was still creating new works - happiest when making 'joyful' art and always innovating with his iPad. His art has sold for millions. In a film originally broadcast in April 2025, ahead of his biggest show ever opening in Paris, the Bradford-born star talks to Katie Razzall about growing old, a recent visit from the King and his two big loves - smoking and painting.

programme.title: Hockney: Double Portrait
programme.desc: An intimate documentary profile of artist David Hockney as he returns to the theme of double portraits, three decades after some of his most iconic works, like Mr and Mrs Clark and Percy. From 2003.

programme.title: Face to Face
programme.desc: In an interview first broadcast in 1996, artist David Hockney talks to Jeremy Isaacs about painting, designing for the opera and his growing deafness.

programme.title: American Visions
programme.desc: Robert Hughes considers America during the 1920s and 30s, including the skyscraper as the image of modernism, the Depression, regionalist art and black art.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest news from the BBC.

programme.title: Tech Now
programme.desc: Andrew Rogers visits a former quarry where new tech is being tested to help people stay underwater for longer. Joe Tidy reports on a huge U-turn for big tech as Instagram stops end-to-end encryption. Franchesca Hashemi explains what you need to know about the lithium-ion batteries in the tech you use every day. And Nick Kwek meets the tech entrepreneur trying to decode animal language.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest news from the BBC.

programme.title: China's Greatest Treasures
programme.desc: Tea is a national drink in China. But it's far more than just a drink for Chinese people. Tea as a culture became fashionable in China during the Tang Dynasty. In this episode, British historian Alastair Sooke discovers how this simple drink has been transformed over centuries into an important social institution, and how it continues to be an essential part of Chinese culture today.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest news from the BBC.

programme.title: The Big Cases
programme.desc: How the husband of Scotland's longest-serving first minister, Nicola Sturgeon, embezzled £400,000 from the party he ran - using a paper trail of lies to cover up a spending spree on luxury goods, from a motor home to coffee machines and even toilet seats. Peter Murrell was one of the most powerful figures in Scottish politics, operating away from the spotlight as chief executive of Scotland's ruling party. But behind the scenes, police pieced together a scandal - which ended in a dawn raid - involving hundreds of thousands of pounds in missing money. He pleaded guilty, while his estranged wife, the party's former leader, denied all knowledge. From the corridors of Holyrood to the highest ranks of the SNP, loyalties fractured as Mr Murrell's reputation collapsed. With insider testimony, analysis and extraordinary revelations from the case, this is a story of power, money and betrayal at the heart of modern Scottish politics.

programme.title: The Travel Show
programme.desc: On the 250th anniversary of the Declaration of Independence, Dwayne Fields travels across the USA, exploring how the document's famous promise - the pursuit of happiness - is being lived today. In part one of two, Dwayne finds out how the Great Migration has impacted Chicago, tries America's highest zip line in Colorado before saddling up to see how tourism has transformed the lives of Utah's iconic cowboys.

programme.title: AI Decoded
programme.desc: As governments and businesses confront their reliance on US tech giants, AI Decoded explores whether Britain can build its own AI future - and how quickly it must act. Now, a sudden decision from Washington is sending shockwaves through the global AI industry, restricting access to some of America's most advanced systems - even for its allies. For UK companies, it raises a stark question: what happens when the technology you depend on is no longer available? Presenter Marc Cieslak is joined by Yang Li of East London startup Cosine, now racing to build a 'sovereign' British alternative as demand surges following the US clampdown. Bloomberg's Katrina Manson also joins to unpack the national security stakes, alongside co-host Stephanie Hare.

programme.title: FIFA World Cup 2026
programme.desc: Live coverage as New Zealand play Belgium in their final Group G match at BC Place, Vancouver. The All Whites of New Zealand have failed to make it out of the group stage or win a match in their two previous appearances. They lost all three games in 1982 and drew all three in 2010. Their talisman is Nottingham Forest striker Chris Wood. Belgium enjoyed their best World Cup finish when they came third at the 2018 tournament in Russia, but they followed it up with a group stage exit in 2022. This will be their 15th appearance in the competition. No other European team has qualified for as many tournaments without winning the trophy. Their key players are likely to be Thibaut Courtois, Kevin De Bruyne and Romelu Lukaku.

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: The latest news, sport, business and weather from the BBC's Breakfast team.

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Ben Lippett and Ravinder Bhogal, and by special guest Jesse Tyler Ferguson. Drinks expert Olly Smith picks the wines to go with the studio dishes, and there are some great moments from the BBC food archive.

programme.title: Anna Haugh’s Big Irish Food Tour
programme.desc: Anna visits the stunning Glens of Antrim, where garden designer Diarmuid Gavin spent magical family holidays as a child. He shows Anna a garden he created to celebrate the King’s coronation. They visit a Ballycastle farm growing Ireland's beloved potato, where they are put to work during potato-planting season. Further up the glen, Anna finds inspiration at Broughgammon Farm, which ensures a high quality of life for goats and calves otherwise discarded by the dairy industry.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A pink bathroom with brown carpeting in Coventry has Dion looking for a sledgehammer. He also visits Doncaster to look around a mid-terrace. Martel is just outside Newcastle, where there’s work to be done to bring another property up to date.

programme.title: BBC Weekend News
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: BBC Weather
programme.desc: The latest weather forecast.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp and the teams go shopping in Hele, near Exeter, with experts Danny Sebastian and John Cameron. Natasha learns more about wheelwrights.

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore tries to smash it with a fish tank transformation, and signwriter Joel puts a new spin on two old record cabinets.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: With a budget of £400,000, an ambitious young couple from the West Midlands has the help of Sonali Shah to find a renovation project and a couple of acres of land in the Welsh Borders. Sonali also heads to Hay-on-Wye to meet a couple who escaped London in 2022 and are in the process of transforming a Grade II listed building into their new home.

programme.title: The Witches
programme.desc: A young boy and his grandmother have a run-in with a coven of witches and their leader. Based on the Roald Dahl novel.

programme.title: BBC Weekend News
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: BBC London
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: BBC Weather
programme.desc: The latest weather forecast.

programme.title: Bridge of Lies
programme.desc: Ross Kemp presents the high-stakes quiz, joined by a celebrity team of comedians – Rosie Jones, Ed Byrne, Nabil Abdulrashid and Harriet Kemsley – as they take on the Bridge of Lies in support of their chosen charities. One by one, they all face the Bridge, choosing who faces each crossing depending on the question category. Each Bridge crossing has 32 potential steps, including 22 truths and ten lies. They must cross the Bridge by stepping on the truths and avoiding the lies, earning money for each correct answer. Step on a lie, and the Bridge will halve their money. Step on three lies, and they are out of the game. There is always a safe path across the Bridge. They just have to find it. Once they have each faced the Bridge on their own, those who have been successful will join forces to face the final crossing for a chance to take home the cash.

programme.title: Blankety Blank
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh hosts as a panel of six celebrities fill in the missing blanks and help win some brilliant prizes for some very lucky contestants. This week, he’s joined by Carol Vorderman, Clara Amfo, Johnny Vegas, Judi Love, Prue Leith and Asim Chaudhry.

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: A celebrity special edition of the general knowledge quiz in which four teams try to come up with the answers that no-one else could think of. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and co-host Richard Osman and featuring Curtis Pritchard, AJ Pritchard, Tom Malone, Karim Zeroual, Shanaze Reade, Oli Bell, Fern Brady and Alison Spittle.

programme.title: The Weakest Link
programme.desc: Romesh Ranganathan is back quizzing eight celebrities who are all after a potential prize pot of £50,000 for their charity. Initially working together, the celebrities must vote off The Weakest Link each round until just two remain in the head-to-head. But only one can claim the prize. Celebrity contestants in this episode are Keith Duffy, Rachel Riley, Amy Gledhill, Ching He Huang, Konan, Rory Bremner, Bonnie Langford and Chris Harris.

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: In the aftermath of the complaint, can Dylan make things right with Stevie and Matty?

programme.title: Two Weeks in August
programme.desc: The group have been kicked out of the villa, and Zoe’s disappearance prompts a frantic search. Nat’s co-dependency with Jacob is challenged when unexpected guests trigger an awkward run-in with the local police. Unable to face up to her actions, Zoe is on a dangerous quest for answers. What is her connection to this island? Can she ever go back to who she was? And even if she could, is it more important to be happy or good?

programme.title: Two Weeks in August
programme.desc: Zoe is looking to a future with Solomon, but her awakening seems to have deadly consequences as a wildfire threatens the island. Not everyone will make it home, and none of them can go back to who they were. Jacob can’t outrun his problems, and Nat must choose her future. Losing Zoe will drive Dan to take dangerous risks and force them both to confront the truth about who they are and what they want. Zoe will have to make a painful decision to step into her life anew.

programme.title: BBC Weekend News
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: BBC Weather
programme.desc: The latest weather forecast.

programme.title: How Are You? It's Alan (Partridge)
programme.desc: Continuing his quest to fix the nation’s mind, Alan posits a question - does talking help, or is it better to just shut your mouth? - and uses it to help the meek speak.

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes host the compulsive, play-along music quiz. Contestants simply have to name hit songs and artists to win a possible £10,000, but can they stay cool under pressure? The teams are best friends Izzy and Ellie from Sheffield, husband and wife Joy and Joe from Nottingham, and dad and son Tom and Paul from Liverpool, all hoping to hit the right note and win a chance to bag that cash prize. Get ready to play along and shout out the answers at the telly!

programme.title: FIFA World Cup 2026
programme.desc: Live coverage of Colombia against Portugal in Group J in Miami on the final day of group games. Portugal are at their seventh consecutive World Cup and reached the quarter-finals in Qatar four years ago. Forty-one-year-old Cristiano Ronaldo remains the talisman of manager Roberto Martinez’s squad, which also includes Manchester United captain Bruno Fernandes. Colombia failed to qualify for Qatar 2022. Their best performance came in 2014, when they reached the quarter-finals, thanks to six goals from star midfielder James Rodriguez.

programme.title: FIFA World Cup 2026
programme.desc: Live coverage as Jordan play Argentina in their final Group J match at the Dallas Stadium. World Cup debutants Jordan face the biggest match in their history against reigning World champions Argentina, led by the great Lionel Messi.

programme.desc: The latest political news, interviews and debate.

programme.title: Prayer and Reflection
programme.desc: A traditional Tibetan Buddhist prayer service from the beautiful Kagyu Samye Ling Monastery in Scotland. The Abbot, Lama Yeshe Rinpoche, welcomes viewers to the retreat service, showcasing their daily sessions of meditation and teachings designed to support inner peace and clarity. Experience the powerful Mahakala prayers along with over 150 participants. These traditional Tibetan Buddhist prayers, filled with chanting, mantra recitation and symbolic offerings, are intended to clear obstacles, purify the environment and cultivate compassion and positivity. The chanted prayers are accompanied by live traditional instruments and these, along with offerings, give a unique insight into the deep spiritual rhythm of Tibetan prayer and reflection.

programme.title: The Boss Baby
programme.desc: Seven-year-old Tim Templeton's idyllic childhood is disrupted by the unexpected arrival of a baby brother, robbing him of his position of centre of attention. He soon realises that his brother is not all that he seems and has arrived on a special mission to save the world from a dastardly scheme cooked up by the CEO of Puppy Company.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning and the team pay a visit to Grey Abbey in Northern Ireland. Experts Ben Cooper and Charles Hanson lend a hand to the teams as they race around the high street pursuing potential purchases for profit at auction.

programme.title: BBC Weekend News
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: Detailed weather forecast.

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Claire McCollum visits a care village in Chester which incorporates a nursery, to explore the joys of connecting across generations and to see how faith plays a key part for many.

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers have their say on the BBC’s television programmes. Whether you’ve seen something you’ve loved or loathed on the BBC, get in touch with your point of view. Email: pov@bbc.co.uk X: @bbcPoV Facebook: Search for BBC Points of View

programme.title: Lifeline
programme.desc: Jeff Brazier presents an appeal on behalf of the Katie Piper Foundation, a charity that helps survivors of burns and trauma recover and rebuild their lives by offering physical, psychological and family support.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton border-hops between Herefordshire and its neighbouring counties today as he helps a buyer from Guernsey to find her perfect party property in the countryside for a budget of £500,000. Whilst in the area, Alistair also pays a home visit to fellow Escape to the Country presenter Jules Hudson, where they reminisce about their time on the show and ponder what the future holds for the countryside housing market.

programme.title: The Americas
programme.desc: A look at the West Coast, where the Pacific Ocean shapes life in unexpected ways. Sea wolves roam the shorelines, hunting along the beaches and adapting to both the land and sea. Off Vancouver Island, a giant Pacific octopus sacrifices herself to protect her eggs, a rare and dramatic act to preserve the species. In Monterey Bay, sea otters navigate powerful waves to reach mussels, showing bold and seldom-seen behaviour. In the redwood forests of California, wandering salamanders leap from the treetops, an extraordinary survival tactic only recently discovered. Beneath the surface, deep ocean canyons are home to strange and diverse creatures. And off the coast of California, male blue whales race and roll at high speed. In all likelihood, they are competing to attract a mate – a spectacle never seen before. From the shores to the ocean depths, the West Coast is full of remarkable wildlife stories and surprising behaviours.

programme.title: The Americas
programme.desc: Patagonia, an untamed wilderness at the edge of the Americas. In the icy waters off Argentina, rockhopper penguins risk their lives against crashing waves and predatory sea lions to feed their chicks, in never-before-seen footage from Los Estados Island. In Chile's Torres del Paine, a mother puma hunts through the snow, revealing unexpected social behaviours among these elusive cats. High in the trees, male Chilean stag beetles engage in epic battles for mates, filmed in microscopic detail. On the grasslands, in rare paternal behaviour, a Darwin’s rhea raises his brood and even adopts a lost chick. Meanwhile, off the coast of Argentina, a matriarch orca teaches her young the art of beaching to catch seals – behaviour that is rarely filmed. On the shores, colossal elephant seals clash for dominance in brutal encounters, showing the struggle for survival in this unforgiving landscape.

programme.title: Roald Dahl's Matilda the Musical
programme.desc: Musical based on Roald Dahl's classic book. Matilda Wormwood is a little girl with a sharp mind, a vivid imagination and the worst parents in the world. On meeting her inspirational teacher Miss Honey, Matilda begins conjuring her own fantastical tales.

programme.title: BBC Weekend News
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: BBC London
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: BBC Weather
programme.desc: The latest weather forecast.

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Charlotte Smith and Joe Crowley mark the 50th anniversary of the Viking Way, exploring the people, wildlife and history connected by Lincolnshire's famous long-distance footpath. They uncover evidence of Viking settlers, meet the volunteers who maintain the route, visit the Ramblers' Church and witness efforts to protect water voles. Adam Henson explores high-tech strawberry farming, and John Craven launches Countryfile's annual photographic competition.

programme.title: Celebrity Escape to the Country
programme.desc: TV’s Dr Ranj has a serious case of the city blues, but a dose of Escape to the Country and some home-shopping therapy could be the cure. Accompanied by artist and friend Tom, Denise Nurse administers a fabulous selection of property options within reach of the Peak District, including one fit for a king. While on the search, they attempt to work out whose job is harder – a doctor’s or a vet’s.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The show is in Northern Ireland at Belfast’s Botanic Gardens, where Fiona Bruce finds out about the history of this green oasis in the heart of the capital city. Treasures include some early poetry by the great Seamus Heaney and an old metal frame that made motorcycling history and was discovered under pig manure. Ronnie Archer Morgan is delighted to spot the detail on one of his favourite Black Forest sculptures - a wooden bear with an artistic streak - and Marc Allum is thrilled by a speck of dust that is quite literally out of this world. Gordon Foster comes across a medal given to an Edwardian plumber on royal duty, and Steven Moore puts on an impromptu performance, startling visitors with his skill on the ocarina.

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Florence’s undercover role takes a dangerous turn when she finds herself in familiar surroundings, amid a murder investigation. In a race against time, can Neville solve the murder?

programme.title: Morning Live

programme.title: Fraud Squad
programme.desc: A council employee falsifies paperwork to steal over £900,000 from a local authority, and a small homewares store turns out to be the front for a huge counterfeit goods operation.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Programme in which experts uncover the various tricks of the property auction trade

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross uncorks an episode of the show fizzing with fun and excitement at the South of England Showground in West Sussex. Among the stalls at the Ardingly Antiques Fair, he quizzes punters about a mystery metal object. While they ponder its purpose, two teams of antiques hunters search for three profitable items to take to auction. On the red team are friends Alex and Ollie, skippered by expert Jonathan Pratt. Against them are the blues, pals Lesley and Rachel, who are shopping with expert Kate Bliss. Early in their shop, Kate spots a silk handbag that looks as if it's art deco but on closer inspection is revealed to be something later, so it goes back in the cabinet. Over with the reds, the lads spy an Edwardian desk set, but Jonathan doesn’t think it’s very practical, so it fails to make the grade. With minutes left on the clock, one team relaxes with a cake and a cuppa, while the other rushes to bag their last buy. In Buckinghamshire, auctioneer Hugo Lemon is ready and waiting to sell the teams’ items, but can he hammer out profits for either of them?

The fight is on for Mrs Brown's good reputation - it's a shame she lost that long ago!

programme.desc: Manchester-based comedy starring Johnny Vegas as small-time dope dealer Moz. Nicki is unhappy because her saucy polaroids have disappeared, while a face from her past has resurfaced. Moz is happy because he is dealing some great new gear and Colin has sold him some saucy polaroids.

programme.title: Ideal
programme.desc: Manchester-based sitcom starring Johnny Vegas as small-time dope dealer Moz. It's Moz's birthday, and Nicki has organised a party. Everyone is having fun - apart, that is, from Moz.

programme.title: Ideal
programme.desc: Sitcom set in Manchester, starring Johnny Vegas as small-time dope dealer Moz. Nicki decides on a pregnancy test, Moz is upset by the return of the severed hand, and Craig gets to bond with the volatile Cartoon Head.

programme.title: Ideal
programme.desc: Manchester-based comedy series starring Johnny Vegas as small-time dope dealer Moz. All Moz has on his mind is the dead body he needs to get rid of. Meanwhile, Nicki has some important news to impart.

programme.title: Doctor Who (2005–2022)
programme.desc: 'Where is the Doctor?' When the skies of Earth are frozen by a mysterious alien force, Clara needs her friend. But where is the Doctor, and what is he hiding from? As past deeds come back to haunt him, old enemies will come face to face, and for the Doctor and Clara, survival seems impossible.

programme.title: Doctor Who (2005–2022)
programme.desc: Trapped and alone on the terrifying planet Skaro, the Doctor is at the heart of the evil Dalek Empire - no sonic, no TARDIS, nobody to help. With his greatest temptation before him, can the Doctor resist? And will there be mercy?

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: Gary is preparing for the grand reopening of the restaurant. Putting on his best chef whites, he has never looked so attractive to Miranda. Will Stevie persuade her to say what she feels? Or will Penny get in the way as she excitedly stalks her poster boy, Raymond Blanc?

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: A fed-up Miranda makes a big life decision: she's going travelling. She has packed her bags and is leaving, this time for good. Stevie is devastated, and Penny is too wrapped up in renewing her wedding vows to notice. Only Gary can stop her going, but how does he really feel?

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: Finding it impossible to have any privacy at their tiny motel home, Johnny and Moira take up Roland’s offer to go to his cabin to rekindle their romance. With her parents out of the motel, Alexis hopes to use the opportunity to throw a party. Using her brother to invite some locals over for a games night, Alexis tries to turn the event into an all-out rager. The night falls apart when the Rose siblings begin to do what they do best - fight with each other.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: Moira is offered her first screen role in years as the ad spokesperson for a local fruit wine company. Hurt that he’s not involved, Johnny tries to take over the shoot, and Moira eventually asks him to leave. Meanwhile, David is suffering from a mysterious illness and must visit the only medic in town, the local vet, for help. Alexis finds that her feelings for Mutt are becoming uncomfortably strong, especially when they are partnered at a pairs yoga session.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: David agrees to go on a turkey hunt with Stevie and some local Creekers to show off his manliness. He must somehow manage to prove himself despite his lack of courage, strength and ability. Meanwhile, Moira’s stress level is through the roof, so Jocelyn takes her to a so-called spa in Elmdale. Things go badly when she ends up coming out looking just like Jocelyn. Alexis starts a relationship with the only normal man in Schitt’s Creek, the young veterinarian Ted.

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: With Dermot and Maria's wedding just weeks away, the Brown household is in crisis. Agnes Brown's son Dermot wants to ask his childhood friend Buster to be best man. But Agnes has set her heart on Mark Brown, her eldest son, being Dermot's best man. And Buster is a crook, of whom both the strong-willed Maria and Mrs Brown strongly disapprove. On top of this, Mrs Brown's close friend and neighbour Winnie is distraught. Her husband Jacko has been rushed to hospital and cannot possibly afford to pay for the operation he urgently requires. And, needing cash for his wedding, Dermot has allowed himself to get embroiled in one of Buster's dodgy moneymaking scams. It's time for Mrs Brown to exercise all her diplomatic skills, starting with 'accidentally' letting Maria know all about Dermot's best man decision.

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Comedy about a brash Irish matriarch. Mrs Brown's hopes of impressing the bride's mother hit trouble when she fails to receive an invitation to Maria's hen party.

programme.title: Only Child
programme.desc: Actor Richard returns home from London to visit his dad, Ken, a year on from the passing of his mum, Jean. But on arriving, he realises his dad may need more help than he'd originally thought.

programme.title: Only Child
programme.desc: Richard decides the time has come to clear Ken’s house of all his junk – or as Ken calls it, ‘his memories’. As Ken’s suspicions over Richard's true motives grow, the arrival of Mr Oddjob, local handyman and Richard’s former school nemesis, complicates matters further.

programme.title: Only Child
programme.desc: Concerned that Ken is spending too much time on his own, Richard offers to take him to the local golf club, unaware that he’s about to play a much bigger part in integrating his dad back into the community than he'd originally intended.

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Jonny falls for Janet's friend Louise, until his obsession is cured in a revolting manner, Janet decides to become a stand-up comedian, and Gaz attempts to manipulate Donna.

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Jonny wants some excitement in his life, and when Janet reveals some remarkable news, he gets rather more than he bargained for. Gaz and Donna have a communication problem - she wants to talk and he wants sex. Louise is simply desperate to prove that she is not an idiot.

programme.title: FIFA World Cup 2026
programme.desc: Relive England's controversial exit at the 2010 World Cup.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Bea drives a wedge between Honey and Billy, the Fox-Truemans receive upsetting news, and Suki struggles with Vinny.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Suki has a run-in with Nicola, and Billy pleads his case.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The pressure mounts for Eve and Suki on the day of their adoption hearing. Phil gives Billy some words of advice.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Nicola and family celebrate half Christmas. Suki and Eve’s moment of happiness is short-lived

programme.title: Challengers
programme.desc: A love triangle forms between three tennis players: formidable former prodigy Tashi, her champion husband Art and his former best friend Patrick, who is also Tashi's ex.

programme.title: I Kissed a Girl
programme.desc: The girls who like girls are back and so is pop icon Dannii Minogue, who is playing Cupid once more to ten single girls who’ve been matched up to meet for the first time… with a kiss. That’s right, no small talk, no swiping, just a kiss. A kiss that could be the start of something incredible. But the path to love is never straight, and in this masseria, everyone’s a possibility. And this summer, Cupid’s lined up something extra special to test the girls’ chemistry, straight off the bat. In true I Kissed a Girl style, a Neon Nights-themed ‘kiki’ sets the wheels in motion for more romance, drama and chaos than ever before. Get ready for the return of the UK’s first queer dating show and a steamy Italian summer to remember!

programme.title: I Kissed a Girl
programme.desc: As the girls settle into their hot queer summer, the sparks are already flying around the masseria. In true U-Haul fashion, things move fast, with some girls already exploring connections outside of their match. And, when Dannii ships three new girls in, there’s even more to unpack. A dinner party dials up the drama, where it’s revealed that ‘girl code’ has officially gone out of the window.

programme.title: OnlyFans: Inside the Machine
programme.desc: Behind the billion-pound success of OnlyFans lies a hidden ecosystem of exploitation. A BBC Current Affairs investigation uncovers allegations of exploitation, coercion and violence committed by some of the hundreds of agents who manage many of the platform’s creators as well as the potential for modern slavery. Through survivor testimony, undercover access to a managers’ chat forum and expert legal analysis, the film exposes an industry where young women are trapped, threatened and profited from - raising urgent questions about accountability at the heart of one of Britain's most lucrative tech companies.

programme.title: Glow Up: Britain's Next Make-Up Star
programme.desc: The MUAs are booked to create summer party looks for global retailer H&M Beauty for an online campaign that will be seen by millions. Joining Val and Dom is guest judge and global creative director for the brand Marjorie Lacombe-Jelinek. The MUAs have just two hours to design and create party looks that focus on the brand's eyeliners, eye shadows and stand-out lip colours. Their looks and models are then screen-tested by the judges to see who has hit the brief and created the strongest look, which will then go on to be shot and featured in the global summer party campaign. This week’s creative brief is all about Seasons of Change. The MUAs are challenged to create a look inspired by a time of change in their own life. The judges want them to design bold, creative and conceptual looks in two and a half hours. Finally, the judges decide which two MUAs will go head-to-head in the Face Off elimination, a make-up skill drill under intense time pressure, after which, one MUA packs up their make-up kit and leaves the competition.

programme.title: Glow Up: Britain's Next Make-Up Star
programme.desc: The six remaining MUAs are thrown into the glossy world of fashion editorials and booked on a job for legendary music magazine Rolling Stone UK, where they must design make-up looks for Grammy-nominated DJ and producer Jayda G. Working alongside the judges are the magazine’s editor-in-chief, Cliff Joannou, and guest judge Hector Espinal, Fenty Beauty’s Global make-up artist and one of Rhianna’s personal MUAs. Each MUA is assigned a female model and mood board, and the brief for today’s feature is 90s raver. The stakes are high, as only one look will be chosen for the editorials feature/digital cover. For this week’s Creative Brief, the MUAs are challenged to produce a look that resembles a statue of how they would like to be remembered. It must include the face, head and shoulders and at least one 3D element. The two MUAs judged to have produced the weakest looks go head-to-head in the Face Off elimination, and the one who least impresses packs up their make-up kit and leaves the competition.

programme.title: FIFA World Cup 2026
programme.desc: Relive a hugely dramatic World Cup quarter-final at Lusail Stadium.

programme.title: The Catch Up
programme.desc: A round-up of the day's news.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: In the final programme from South Africa, Steve braves the Cape's rough seas to get close to the great white shark. Frustration grows as the bad weather looks like scuppering his plans. Meanwhile, Steve gets to go out scavenging with the most fearless animal in the world. He also gets bitten, scratched and generally mauled by almost all the animals he meets on a trip to an animal sanctuary.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The Fox-Truemans rally together. Priya questions Max’s intentions. Will tries to help Billy in his battle with Bea.

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson subjects the Skoda Yeti to a remarkably thorough road test, while James May makes a rare visit to the test track in the insane Aerial Atom V8. Meanwhile, Richard Hammond celebrates the incredible evolution of the Porsche 911 by racing a modern Turbo Cabriolet against its oldest relative and the power of gravity. Plus there is more heated studio debate with the return of the Cool Wall, and another celebrity guest takes their turn in the Reasonably Priced Car.

programme.title: Football’s New Generation
programme.desc: The intimate story of England's newest recruits, charting their rise from grassroots heroes to Europe's biggest stage. Featuring Cole Palmer, Kobbie Mainoo, Ollie Watkins and Marc Guehi, the documentary reveals their personal stories for the first time, with contributions from their loved ones, family and friends, as well as the veterans who mentored them to the world stage. The film charts their paths from humble beginnings, revealing for the first time the personal battles that culminated in a new breed of players representing England at Euro 2024.

programme.title: The Ministry of Ungentlemanly Warfare
programme.desc: Gus March-Phillips leads a ragbag of unruly agents in Operation Postmaster, a dangerous mission to sabotage Nazi U-boats in west Africa during the Second World War.

programme.title: Fake Friend: The Ticket Scammer
programme.desc: How a Somerset high-flyer turned his charm and connections into tools of deception – conning his school friends and hundreds of others while living a life of glamour himself.

programme.title: Gods of Tennis
programme.desc: It’s 1990, and Martina Navratilova – Czech-born but representing the USA - has the chance to become the most decorated singles player in Wimbledon history. In comparison, her famous rival Chris Evert is America’s sweetheart. Evert is heralded as the perfect celebrity athlete, pigeonholed based on her looks, but she is Navratilova’s most formidable opponent, with a reputation for incredible mental toughness. This episode tells the story of their intense rivalry, but at the heart of the story is Navratilova’s search for acceptance – which culminates in an ambition to become the greatest player to ever grace Centre Court.

programme.title: FIFA World Cup 2026
programme.desc: Live coverage as Egypt play their final Group G match against Iran in Seattle. In three previous attempts, Egypt have failed to make it past the first phase and lost all three of their group games when they last qualified for a World Cup back in 2018. Iran are at their fourth consecutive World Cup, having qualified on six occasions in total, but like their opponents, they are yet to contest a knockout game.

programme.title: This is BBC Two
programme.desc: Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.

programme.title: Piripenguins
programme.desc: Nervous about public speaking, Looph builds a robot to deliver a science talk in her place, but her friends are determined to help her do it herself.

programme.title: Big Lizard
programme.desc: When the buggy gets a flat tyre, Dad, Cosima and Big Lizard are stranded. Big Lizard’s playful nature helps reveal a creative solution to their problem.

programme.title: The Weasy Family
programme.desc: The ducklings paint a picture, which Weasy learns to appreciate, but it ends up in the possession of the farmer, who has also fallen in love with the painting. Weasy and the ducklings have to perform a heist to get it back.

programme.title: Super Happy Magic Forest
programme.desc: Twinkle uses her excellent pocket beast card skills when on a quest to rescue the missing Council of Happiness from Grackle the Giant.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The Lemmings adopt a cute insect that eats wood. And what's made of wood? The cabin!

programme.title: Duck and Frog
programme.desc: When his beloved plant is sold by mistake, Duck must lose his job at the garden centre in order to retrieve it from a top-security greenhouse.

programme.title: Pokémon Horizons
programme.desc: Memories come flooding back when Orla is asked to build a new airship.

programme.title: Winx Club - The Magic Is Back
programme.desc: Following a scary encounter with a magic creature on her 16th birthday, Bloom discovers that she has magical powers. She is rescued by two fairies from Alfea School for Fairies and Witches.

programme.title: Gladiators: Epic Pranks
programme.desc: Dynamite, Nitro, Comet and Giant go head-to-head at a restaurant full of diners - but will beatboxing, singing burgers, flat cola and a leaping salmon be a dare too far?

programme.title: Newsround
programme.desc: Topical news magazine for children.

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Football freestyle star Ben Nuttall goes head-to-head with a rising young champion in an epic skills battle that is packed with tricks and plenty of surprises! Joel sets out on a mission to become a superhero - the first two challenges he faces are flying and attempting to create his very own superhero costume!

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: At Longmeadow, Monty is in full summer mode - revamping and refreshing borders to keep the garden looking its best right through the season. He turns his attention to the cucurbit family, planting melons and squash, shares a simple trick to extend the flowering of hardy geraniums, and sows perennial seeds for spectacular colour next year. Arit Anderson heads to south east London to discover the striking transformation of a garden that was once a derelict space into a bold, contemporary community garden powered by creativity, collaboration and clever planting. Rachel de Thame explores how self-seeding plants can be carefully choreographed to create a beautifully natural, flowing design. There’s a passionate grower dedicated to the National Collection of penstemons, and in the Yorkshire Dales, a florist and lifelong gardener shares a deeply personal story of resilience - finding strength, purpose and renewed creativity through rebuilding a garden after a life-changing diagnosis.

programme.title: Mr Malcolm's List
programme.desc: Spurned by one of society's wealthiest and most eligible bachelors, husband-seeking Julia Thistlewaite is outraged to discover she does not live up to Mr Malcolm's list of required attributes in a potential wife. Seeking revenge, she invites her best friend, the daughter of an impoverished clergyman, to ensnare Mr Malcolm.

programme.title: Andi Oliver’s Fabulous Feasts
programme.desc: Andi Oliver’s in Burnley to supersize an annual cricket match between members of the local mosques and clergy. Set up to overcome the divisions that led to race riots in 2001, Burnley’s imams and vicars have been staging the cricketing contest every year, building bridges between their communities. This year they want the event to be the biggest yet, with larger crowds and better food, showing that Burnley is a united town with a positive future. To make sure it’s a success, Andi wants to devise the ultimate cricket tea, sourcing the best local produce and tracking down talented chefs from the wonderfully diverse backgrounds that make up this old mill town. She also wants to add some razzmatazz to the event to draw in the crowds and ensure it's an event that appeals to all. But will her attempt to make this a premier date in the Lancashire cricketing calendar, galvanising Burnley folk for years to come, be stumped by an incoming storm?

programme.title: Nigel Slater's Dish of the Day
programme.desc: Nigel Slater raids his cupboards, fridge and vegetable patch to create a week's worth of delicious meals. In this edition, he demonstrates how to make our ingredients go just that little bit further. Whether it's adding grated beetroot to make a tasty meatball supper feed the masses or putting the spotlight on a small garden harvest to make a sensuous pudding, Nigel's clever tricks and inspired ideas show that you can often make bountiful feasts with apparently few ingredients.

programme.title: The Railway Children
programme.desc: 1904, England. When their father is mysteriously arrested, the Waterbury children and their mother downsize and move to the Yorkshire countryside. Bobbie, Phyllis and Peter discover the local steam railway, and on their adventures, they befriend the station porter, Mr Perks, and try to discover the truth about their father. Lionel Jeffries's beloved film version of ES Nesbit's classic book.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Flog It! comes from Margam Country Park in South Wales. Experts Mark Stacey and Catherine Southon choose items to take to auction. Mark Stacey meets someone with an eye for a bargain and Catherine is taken by a wonderful pink rolls Royce! Presenter Paul Martin visits the birthplace of one of his heroes, a Welshman who took Hollywood by storm.

programme.title: Close Encounters of the Third Kind
programme.desc: Out on a late night job alone, power repairman Roy Neary has an encounter with an alien spacecraft and becomes consumed with the desire to see it again. The government denies it, but other people have been seeing the UFOs and having strange experiences, too. Soon, Roy is on a one-way mission to expose the truth.

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Top ceramicist and star of The Great Pottery Throw Down Keith Brymer Jones takes on Esme Young, judge of The Great British Sewing Bee, in a competition to find the most profitable antique. Accompanied by antiques experts Roo Irvine and Charles Hanson, the celebrity competitors comb through some of Glasgow’s finest antique shops with a £400 budget and a classic car to take them between stops.

programme.title: Cricket: Today at the Test
programme.desc: Day 3 highlights from the third Test match between England and New Zealand.

programme.title: Glastonbury
programme.desc: With Glastonbury Festival taking a well-deserved fallow year break in 2026, this deep dive into the archives revisits 40 precious performances from Worthy Farm. This extended celebration spans from when the festival was first televised in 1994 right through to 2025, with appearances from the likes of Adele, Blondie, Blur, Dolly Parton, Elton John, Florence + The Machine, Johnny Cash, Kylie Minogue, Lizzo, Oasis, Olivia Rodrigo and Robert Smith, Paul McCartney, Pet Shop Boys, Pulp, REM, Radiohead, Robbie Williams, Stormzy, Sugababes and The Rolling Stones. Plus rarely seen performance footage of Amy Winehouse, Ed Sheeran and Olivia Dean.

programme.title: Glastonbury
programme.desc: Clara Amfo and Lauren Laverne are live from Worthy Farm to introduce 2025's Glastonbury Festival legend, pop rock icon Rod Stewart. The British singer, songwriter and producer, who celebrated another number one album last year, will step out onto the Pyramid Stage for the first time in over 20 years, having previously headlined the festival in 2002. With his trademark raspy, soulful voice and armed with a huge back catalogue of hits, there’s no doubt he will have the crowd singing along and swinging their hips to songs like Maggie May, You Wear It Well and Do Ya Think I’m Sexy? as he promises to entertain the Glastonbury teatime revellers.

programme.title: FIFA World Cup 2026
programme.desc: Live coverage as World Cup debutants Uzbekistan play their final Group K game against the Democratic Republic of Congo in Atlanta. Uzbekistan are managed by 2006 World Cup-winning captain Fabio Cannavaro, with Manchester City’s Abdukodir Khusanov in the centre of their defence. Last appearing in the 1974 World Cup as Zaire, the Democratic Republic of Congo enjoyed an impressive qualifying campaign for the 2026 tournament, drawing in Senegal before knocking out both Cameroon and Nigeria in the African play-offs.

programme.title: FIFA World Cup 2026
programme.desc: Live coverage as Algeria play Austria in their final Group J match at Kansas City Stadium. Algeria have made it out of the group stage just once in four previous appearances, taking eventual champions Germany into extra time in the last 16 in 2014. These are their first finals since then. They are due to be captained by former Leicester and Manchester City winger Riyad Mahrez. Austria have qualified for the first time since 1998. It’s their eighth World Cup appearance, with their best result coming with a third-place finish in 1954. Their head coach is former Manchester United interim Ralf Rangnick.

programme.title: This is BBC Two
programme.desc: Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.

programme.title: The A to Z of TV Gardening
programme.desc: Carol Kirkwood takes an alphabetical journey through the world of gardening, getting inspiration and advice from some of the BBC's most popular garden presenters and programmes. In this episode, she is exploring subjects that all begin with the letter H.

programme.title: Glorious Gardens from Above
programme.desc: The gardens of England's northernmost county are the destination for Christine Walkden. At Cragside she helps with some tree planting and meets fern queen Jennifer. At Lindisfarne she hears how some over-exuberant Edwardian planting is undermining the castle foundations. And we hear the story of Britain's forgotten genius, engineer William Armstrong.

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Away from Hampshire's famous ports and bustling harbours lies a wild world of woodland, heathland and chalk streams. Matt Baker and Charlotte Smith meet the people working to find, protect and celebrate the county's lesser-spotted wildlife. Charlotte also investigates how proposed changes to visa rules could affect sheep shearing in the UK.

programme.title: Beechgrove Garden
programme.desc: Kirsty plants meconopsis, and Brian is in the 6x8 greenhouse looking at what you can do if you have this standard-sized glasshouse. Also, a visit to a great community garden in the Easterhouse area of Glasgow that is used and enjoyed by a range of groups in the local community, and handy hints. Kirsty and Brian start a new feature that gives 'everything you need to know' plant profiles on some of the country’s most popular garden varieties - from growing patterns and soil preference to pruning plans. Throughout the series are fact files on some of the plants most often found in viewers' plots.

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: With the berry harvest in full swing, Dougie is in the East Neuk of Fife, where he discovers how the strawberry season has extended over the last 20 years. Cammy is in North Lanarkshire meeting the sheep dresser who gets livestock competition-ready for the Royal Highland Show. With the price of wool on the rise, Rosie visits the grading depot in Selkirk to find out what is driving the increasing value of fleeces. Shahbaz is in Moray stepping into the high-tech tree nursery that is the biggest in the UK, growing 19 million saplings a year. And Leanna is in Aberdeen to meet the scent-detection dogs fighting for native red squirrels by sniffing out invasive greys.

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt dishes up a delicious seasonal menu from favourite Saturday Kitchen chefs. Paul Ainsworth tosses Cornish crab with squid ink spaghetti, overnight tomatoes and a good hit of garlic and chilli. Ben Tish cooks up grilled bavette steak marinated in mint, fennel and balsamic vinegar, served with braised courgettes and a fresh hit of gremolata. Jane Dunn has three twists on the classic scone recipe with apples, blueberries, cranberries and white chocolate. Sabrina Ghayour serves ricotta dumplings with a smoked aubergine and roasted red pepper dip, and Georgina Hayden prepares a delicious chicken recipe. And Nick Mohammed tucks into either his food heaven, a trio of Chinese takeaway classics, or his food hell, rice pudding with strawberry compote. Meanwhile, Rick Stein reluctantly shares his favourite kebab shop in London, and the Hairy Bikers find themselves in the middle of a Sardinian party.

programme.title: Mary Berry - Cook and Share
programme.desc: Mary unearths the jewels of the Jurassic Coast, meeting those passionate about its past and developing ingredients to sustain its future, all while sharing her own inspired recipes.

programme.title: Mary Berry - Cook and Share
programme.desc: Mary joins revelers at this year’s Wilderness Festival, where good food takes centre stage, promoting the idea of sharing the cooking experience.

programme.title: Nigel Slater's Simple Suppers
programme.desc: Nigel makes a Mediterranean dish of pepper, tomato and basil pasta, and chestnut-stuffed mushrooms.

programme.title: Wimbledon
programme.desc: A look back at the highlights from Wimbledon 2025.

programme.title: How the West Was Won
programme.desc: Epic western about three generations of a pioneer family, showing how its fortunes fluctuated during the dynamic westward expansion of America during the 19th century. Three top directors each tackle separate episodes of the story, filmed in the short-lived three-camera Cinerama process and with an all-star cast.

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: In North Uist, the MacDonalds are preparing for one of the biggest events in their calendar – walking their 150 pregnant highland cattle from the mainland to the tidal island of Vallay. It’s only accessible by foot for a six-hour window, which means they must race against the tide to get them across. Camy enjoys his favourite pastime and an important earner – primping and preening other farmers’ animals for agricultural shows. And Rachel gets a chance to step into the ring too, with one of Allan’s calves. In the Cairngorms National Park, Jenni is planning for the year ahead with her fledgling alpaca, trekking business. She’s decided a trio of pygmy goats is the perfect addition to the menagerie. Robert isn’t so sure.

programme.title: Athletics: Diamond League
programme.desc: Coverage from the 2026 Diamond League meeting in Paris, France.

programme.title: Cricket: Today at the Test
programme.desc: Day 4 highlights from the third Test match between England and New Zealand.

programme.title: My Epic Camel Adventure with Gordon Buchanan
programme.desc: Gordon Buchanan takes an epic journey across the Gobi Desert on one of the world’s most iconic, yet least understood, animals – camels. Starting in the west of Mongolia, Gordeon crosses blistering sand dunes, endless rocky plains and the treacherous Bumbat Mountain, with only his camels for company. If he is to succeed, he must befriend the camels, learn as much as he can about them, and try to win their trust and respect. Without that, the journey and his survival are in question. Naming camels isn’t something that nomads would do, but as his relationship with them strengthens, he decides to name them Squeaks and Chewy. They are lucky enough to meet some of the other inhabitants of this seemingly desolate landscape, such as wolves, saiga antelopes and yaks. But will he be lucky enough to catch sight of the most endangered bear on the planet – the Gobi Bear? Spending time with different nomads also gives Gordon time to reflect on the appeal of the traditional nomadic way of life, and his own modern existence. Ultimately, he discovers how camels have evolved in the most unusual ways to survive and prosper in one of the most hostile environments on the planet.

programme.title: Murder Trial
programme.desc: The remains of a body are discovered in an abandoned warehouse in Scotland, but the victim, the motive and the cause of death are all unknown. Soon, a shocking and premeditated crime is uncovered as decorator David Barnes stands accused of killing his customer - but nobody knows how he died. In a unique criminal case shrouded in mystery, this programme follows the family’s quest for justice and goes behind the scenes at the Scottish High Court.

programme.title: Back to Black
programme.desc: The life and music of Amy Winehouse, who never did things by halves. The story follows the development of her unique talent and passion - from her teen years through to life in London's Camden Town, her tempestuous relationship with Blake Fielder-Civil and her development into a singular and iconic musician.

programme.title: All Is True
programme.desc: After London's Globe Theatre burns to the ground in 1613, William Shakespeare stops writing and returns to his home town of Stratford-upon-Avon, much to the surprise of his family members, who he has neglected for two decades. Here, Shakespeare faces new challenges as he grapples with the story of his own life and sets out to resolve its final chapter.

programme.title: Expedition with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: Steve takes on four entirely new adventures to some of the remotest parts of the planet in search of precious natural wonders. In the desert kingdom of Saudi Arabia, Steve teams up with one of the world’s best free climbers, Leo Houlding, and former Royal Marine Aldo Kane. Together, they scale the highest peak in the ancient Dedan kingdom to unearth the secrets of this 2,500-year-old civilisation. In Gabon, Steve heads deep into the jungle to find a very special group of gorillas who have learned to trust humans – but who no-one has seen for nearly a year. In Mexico, Steve travels to the Sea of Cortez – known as the world’s aquarium – on a mission to free dive with mobula rays. Although they are protected by law, this incredibly rare marine species is under threat of extinction. In Kamchatka, Steve sets out to explore the Russian tundra in autumn, when brown bears are feasting on salmon. He witnesses a pristine, untouched wilderness where wildlife is unaffected by humans – a glimpse of the world as it should be.

programme.title: The Future with Hannah Fry
programme.desc: From Fukushima to California, Hannah explores whether rewilding can help to heal the environmental wounds humans have inflicted on the planet.

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Strictly dancer Amy Dowden turns amateur sleuth, travelling to west Wales to see if she can discover the truth behind a rumour of murder in her family history.

programme.title: This is BBC Two
programme.desc: Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Chris Hull and Flo Headlam visit Didcot to transform a garden that has been abandoned to the elements into a Bridgerton-inspired garden with a safe play area for a family's three energetic boys.

programme.title: The Travelling Auctioneers
programme.desc: Joyce and her husband Mike spent more than 40 years living together in Guernsey. But after they both faced health challenges, they decided to return to Mike’s beloved home county of Yorkshire for a fresh start. Just months after settling in, tragedy struck when Mike passed away unexpectedly, leaving Joyce heartbroken and surrounded by a lifetime of their shared treasures. Now, with the support of close friends Brian and Adele, Joyce is ready to let go of some of the old items that have filled her new home. She hopes the auction will help her gain some space, while raising money to support her son’s work rescuing stray dogs in Thailand, honouring the couple’s shared love of animals. Auctioneer James Broad and restorer Saf Fakir roll up in West Yorkshire to lend a hand. JB uncovers pocket watches, a Victorian cauldron and a trove of jewellery, while Saf takes on three challenging restorations, including an oak coal scuttle, a writing slope and a rare pair of brass coffin stands. With a big mix of lots heading under the hammer, can JB and Saf help Joyce turn Mike’s collections into a lasting legacy and raise enough to help a cause they cared so deeply about?

programme.title: Tiger Island
programme.desc: In western Nepal, the team are continuing their tiger expedition. As monsoon storms loom and temperatures soar, the big cats are becoming more active at night. Tiger mother Goma is teaching her cubs to become independent, while Mala is struggling to keep her lone surviving cub out of trouble. The crew must adapt to the challenging conditions, deploying military-grade thermal cameras and drones to film the tigers after sunset. Under cover of darkness, the arrival of a huge dominant male presents a rare opportunity to study tiger family dynamics – but how will the male respond when he comes face to face with the cubs?

programme.title: See Hear
programme.desc: An exploration of the experiences of deaf people accessing healthcare across the UK, asking why communication barriers continue to affect patient care despite legal accessibility requirements. Presenter Ahmed Mudawi is joined by Lucy Warnes, chief executive of SignHealth. Together, they discuss the challenges deaf people face when accessing NHS services, the importance of the Accessible Information Standard and the ongoing campaign for equal access to healthcare.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The big political issues of the day.

programme.title: Wimbledon
programme.desc: Live coverage of the Championships at the All England Lawn Tennis Club at Wimbledon.

programme.title: Wimbledon
programme.desc: Live coverage of the Championships at the All England Lawn Tennis Club at Wimbledon.

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson uncovers how the health and fortune of the village was shaped by those in charge of Bamburgh’s famous castle.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The Fox-Truemans rally together. Priya questions Max’s intentions. Will tries to help Billy in his battle with Bea.

programme.title: Sort Your Life Out
programme.desc: Can this family clear out their room of doom to create a magical mermaid bedroom?

programme.title: Lucy Worsley Investigates
programme.desc: On 5 November 1605, a group of radical Catholic conspirators, including Guy Fawkes, came within hours of blowing up the Houses of Parliament. Their aim was to assassinate the Protestant king James I and destroy his government in an attempt to restore England to the Catholic faith. Had it not been stopped, this one moment of violence could have changed the history of the country entirely. The Gunpowder Plot was foiled, but its remembrance was written into law, and Guy Fawkes Night is still celebrated on 5 November. Lucy Worsley investigates what drove this group of men to attempt such an audacious act. She delves into the motivations and mindsets of the Catholic plotters, searching for evidence in their childhoods, the rooms where they secretly met, and where Guy Fawkes finally faced his interrogators.

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul and Bob are in the Lake District to fish at some of the UK’s best-loved beauty spots – Derwentwater and Watendlath Tarn. In a change from the norm, this time Bob has chosen where they go fishing in a poignant trip down memory lane for him - this was where he used to come on camping holidays with his mates, away from parents for the first time.

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Insights and global interviews on the news stories of the day. With Victoria Derbyshire.

programme.title: Duck and Frog
programme.desc: Frog creates chaos in a loo roll factory, leaving Duck covered in toilet paper. Duck just wants to fix the machine, but Frog is convinced he’s a spooky mummy!

programme.title: Duck and Frog
programme.desc: Duck is in charge of a campsite and has to make sure everyone follows the rules. However, Frog turns up and breaks them all - he lets goats loose and has a midnight party in his tent.

Meanwhile, Jude finds a way for Cleo to still go to nationals, with disastrous consequences.

programme.title: Jamie Johnson FC
programme.desc: Hawx fan Jordan dreams of playing in the futsal tournament at the Academy. Meanwhile, Hawx players are pitted against each other, and Charlie faces a difficult decision.

programme.title: Bro's in Control

programme.desc: Reeling from the loss of Kenzie, Richelle asks the Step Inside team to show her their best duets to prove their commitment, and Kingston struggles to set boundaries. programme.title: The Lady Grace Mysteries
